title: New Conversation
context:
Q: what is measles
A: Measles is a highly contagious viral disease that causes fever, cough, runny nose, and a characteristic rash.

Q: what is apple
A: I do not know.

messages.content: Measles is a highly contagious viral disease that causes fever, cough, runny nose, and a characteristic rash.

citations.text: MEASLES Measles (aka, rubeola, red measles or hard measles) is a very communicable viral illness that can be prevented by immunization.
MEASLES Measles is one of the_most_readily transmissible communicable diseases. The communicable period is greatest prior to or just after rash onset.
RATIONALE FOR SURVEILLANCE Measles is targeted for a reduction by 90% for incidence and by 95% for mortality (Q9GPW 6.2). Surveillance for measles evolves with each phase of measles control.
RECOMMENDED CASE DEFINITION Clinical case definition Any person with: e fever, and ¢ maculopapular (i.e. non-vesicular) rash, and « cough, coryza (i.e. runny nose) or conjunctivitis (i.e. red eyes).

GLOSSARY Viruses can grow or reproduce only in living cells. Examples of viruses include hepatitis B, HIV and the common cold. . Infection — When an infectious agent multiplies in the body.

GLOSSARY Antibody — A protein substance produced by the defense system in response to something foreign. Antibodies help protect against infections. Asymptomatic — Without symptoms.
